message:
beads: file fcp-graphql review findings (wd6ei, upp69, jdhaw)

Closing the fcp-graphql client review.

Injection / escaping: no bug at the client layer. GraphqlClient
builds the POST body as {query, variables, operationName} with
variables flowing through serde_json::to_value -- properly encoded,
no concatenation. Callers that format!() query strings with user
input are their own problem.

Connection pool + in-flight dedup findings:

  wd6ei [HIGH] Dedup-map entries leak on caller cancel/panic at
               client.rs:519-538. The remove(&key) line only runs
               on non-cancel, non-panic completion. Once a Shared
               resolves, subsequent same-body callers get the
               cached result forever -- dedup-in-flight silently
               becomes dedup-forever. Memory grows monotonically
               with unique request bodies from cancelled callers.
               Needs a RAII guard or Weak<Shared> map.

  upp69 [LOW]  Dedup keys on u64 DefaultHasher (SipHash fixed
               seed). Collisions let an attacker serve the wrong
               response to a same-hash body. Exploit surface is
               narrow; fix is trivial (key on Vec<u8> or blake3
               digest).

  jdhaw [LOW]  Every GraphqlClient::new / with_config builds a
               fresh HttpClient with its own connection pool.
               Clone shares the pool, but the API surface invites
               per-request construction. Not a leak, a foot-gun.

Also spot-checked subscription.rs: the spawned WebSocket task
terminates on tx.closed(), so dropping the stream rx cleanly ends
the task and closes the ws. OK.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j6ps","title":"[IDEA-22] Device Posture Attestation Enforcement","description":"## Background
The README and RFCs describe optional hardware-backed device posture attestations for high-trust zones, but enforcement is not yet mechanical.

## Goals
- Introduce `DevicePostureAttestation` objects and verification rules.
- Allow zone policies to require hardware-backed keys (TPM/Secure Enclave).
- Fail closed when posture is missing or invalid.

## Non-goals
- Implementing hardware attestation collection itself for every OS in this iteration (focus on verification and policy).

## Approach
- Define canonical schema for posture attestation.
- Extend enrollment validation and zone policy evaluation to require posture.
- Add test fixtures for valid/invalid posture.

## Risks
- Platform variance in attestation formats; keep schema extensible and versioned.

## Success Criteria
- Zones can enforce posture requirements with stable reason codes.
- Tests cover accept/deny paths.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Zones can enforce posture requirement with stable deny reason.
- Enrollment without posture fails closed in required zones.
","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.
[AlienArtifactReview] Updated 2026-02-02T23:58:59.240755+00:00","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:44:58.618987180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:09:54.121377395Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T05:09:54.121247864Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j7w1","title":"[IDEA-11] Streaming Health Signal Unification","description":"# Streaming Health Signal Unification

## Goal
Standardize how streaming connectors report health (heartbeat ack, zombie detection, reconnection) so hosts can interpret status consistently.

## Background / Why
Different connectors currently surface health in ad-hoc ways. A unified model improves monitoring, alerting, and auto-restart behavior.

## Scope
- Define health state transitions for streaming connections.
- Provide helper methods for health updates and log emission.

## Deliverables
- `StreamingHealthState` mapping to `HealthSnapshot`.
- Standard log fields for heartbeat/ack counts.

## Tests
- Unit tests for state transitions.
- Integration tests with a simulated streaming loop.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Health signals are deterministic and consistent across connectors.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add metamorphic tests (encode->decode->encode) plus differential oracle for edge cases.
- Use sequential test budgets with e-value thresholds to stop early when safe.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Parallelize vector generation with deterministic seeds.
- Cache canonical forms to avoid repeated parse cost.
[AlienArtifactReview] Updated 2026-02-02T23:58:59.240755+00:00","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:38:14.343854819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T02:20:13.619298Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T02:20:13.619242Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j7w1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-2zy9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1108,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j7w1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Streaming Health Signal Unification fully implemented: StreamingHealthState in fcp-sdk/src/runtime.rs (serde, last_heartbeat_at, last_ack_at, reconnect_count, missed_heartbeats), HealthTracker with deterministic state transitions (Starting→Healthy→Degraded→Unhealthy→Stopping), HealthSnapshot integration. All tests passing. Closing.","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:20:13Z"}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-jdhaw","title":"[LOW] fcp-graphql GraphqlClient constructors build a fresh HttpClient per call (foot-gun, not a leak)","description":"crates/fcp-graphql/src/client.rs:302-320: both GraphqlClient::new and GraphqlClient::with_config call HttpClientBuilder::new().build() and wrap the result in Arc<HttpClient>. Each constructed GraphqlClient owns its own connection pool.

GraphqlClient implements Clone by sharing the Arc<HttpClient>, so the intended pattern is "construct once, clone everywhere." A caller that instead calls GraphqlClientBuilder::new(url).build() per request will spawn a fresh pool each time; those pools release when the client drops, but HTTP connections held in the pool do not close eagerly.

Not a leak, but the API surface invites the foot-gun. Improvements:

  (a) Accept an Arc<HttpClient> in the builder so callers can share a single pool across many GraphqlClients.
  (b) Add a #[doc] warning on GraphqlClient::new explicitly calling out that each new instance spawns its own pool and recommending Clone.
  (c) Cache-by-endpoint: GraphqlClientBuilder could intern HttpClient instances by (endpoint, tls config, timeout) to share pools across callers. More complex; only if the foot-gun bites in production.

Files: crates/fcp-graphql/src/client.rs:302-320 (constructors), client.rs:193-264 (builder).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T08:00:47.361428Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:00:47.361428Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Patched connectors/gmail/src/main.rs and connectors/openai/src/main.rs to skip whitespace-only stdin lines and wrap malformed JSON in a proper JSON-RPC parse-error envelope with jsonrpc/id:null, added focused main-local regressions, and updated connectors/gmail/src/client.rs to make with_retry_config const so the slice passes focused bin-level clippy. Verification: rustfmt, git diff --check, remote cargo check -p fcp-gmail -p fcp-openai --all-targets, remote cargo test -p fcp-gmail --bin fcp-gmail -- --nocapture, remote cargo test -p fcp-openai --bin fcp-openai -- --nocapture, remote cargo clippy -p fcp-gmail --bin fcp-gmail -- -D warnings, remote cargo clippy -p fcp-openai --bin fcp-openai -- -D warnings. Broad remote cargo clippy -p fcp-gmail -p fcp-openai --all-targets -- -D warnings still fails on unrelated pre-existing warnings in connectors/gmail/tests/migration_acceptance.rs, connectors/gmail/tests/integration.rs, and connectors/openai/src/connector.rs.","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:35:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-k4ka","title":"fcp-graphql: GraphQL Client Infrastructure Library","description":"# fcp-graphql: GraphQL Client Infrastructure Library


## Goal
Provide a shared, typed GraphQL client library for connectors with retries/pagination/subscriptions, mapping errors to the FCP taxonomy and fully mock-tested.

## Overview
Shared GraphQL client library for connectors that interface with GraphQL APIs (Linear, GitHub, Notion, Shopify, etc.).

## Features

### Core Client
- Async GraphQL client with connection pooling
- Query/mutation/subscription support
- Automatic retry with exponential backoff
- Request batching and deduplication
- Query complexity estimation

### Query Building
- Type-safe query builder
- Fragment composition
- Variable handling
- Directive support

### Pagination
- Cursor-based pagination (Relay-style)
- Offset-based pagination
- Automatic page traversal
- Total count estimation

### Subscriptions
- WebSocket transport
- Reconnection handling
- Subscription multiplexing
- Heartbeat management

### Introspection
- Schema introspection caching
- Type validation
- Deprecation warnings

### Error Handling
- GraphQL error parsing
- Partial data handling
- Network error recovery
- Rate limit detection

## Dependencies
- reqwest (HTTP)
- tokio-tungstenite (WebSocket)
- serde_json
- fcp-core

## Consumers
- fcp.linear (GraphQL-only API)
- fcp.github (GraphQL API v4)
- fcp.notion (REST+GraphQL hybrid)
- fcp.shopify (Admin API)

## Testing
- Mock GraphQL server
- Response recording/playback
- Schema validation tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- The functionality described in this bead is implemented with typed schemas + validation (no unstructured “stringly” interfaces).
- Errors map to the FCP error taxonomy with correct retry semantics and no secret/PII leakage.
- Mock-only unit/integration tests cover success paths and common failures and emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ukr33.1","title":"[FCP3/P7.2a] Build the repeatable benchmark harness for cutover-critical flows","description":"Background: the project names aggressive performance goals, but final proof needs a repeatable harness that measures the cutover-critical flows the same way before and after deletion.

Scope: build the repeatable benchmark harness for the cutover-critical flows selected during baseline capture. Preserve environment controls, measurement formats, and enough logging that later comparisons are reproducible.

Acceptance: the repo has one repeatable harness for measuring the agreed cutover-critical flows rather than a collection of ad hoc benchmark runs.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:59:22.163916Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-11T01:44:02.494069Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T01:44:02.493091Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmarks","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","logging","performance","phase-7"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ukr33.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-34q27.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:59:32.993669Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ukr33.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ukr33","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:59:22.163916Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2440,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ukr33.1","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Group benchmark note: performance proof must use one repeatable harness, explicit environment capture, and structured measurement logs. Package comparison thresholds and rationale for any intentional regressions so the performance review is mechanical rather than interpretive.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:23Z"},{"id":2677,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ukr33.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Implemented unified cutover benchmark harness: crates/fcp-conformance/benches/cutover_harness.rs. 6 criterion groups (revocation, protocol, crypto, gossip, serialization, enforcement) covering all cutover-critical flows from the baseline doc. Parametric sizes, structured output via --output-format bencher. Commit a1a30435.","created_at":"2026-04-11T01:44:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ukr33.2","title":"[FCP3/P7.2a] Capture before-and-after performance comparisons and review thresholds","description":"Background: once the harness exists, final proof still needs explicit before-and-after comparisons and review thresholds.

Scope: run the cutover-critical benchmark harness against the preserved pre-cutover baseline and the post-deletion architecture, then package the comparison tables, review thresholds, and explanatory notes for any intentional changes.

Acceptance: performance proof can show not just raw numbers but a defensible before-and-after comparison with explicit pass or review  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-un5y","title":"TEST: Audit Chain Unit Tests (Hash Linking, Checkpoints, DecisionReceipts, Fork Detection)","description":"# TEST: Audit Chain Unit Tests

## Goal
Prove that the audit chain provides tamper-evident history with explainable decisions.

## Test Categories

### AuditEvent Chain
- [ ] Events hash-linked correctly (prev_hash binding)
- [ ] Monotonic seq enforced
- [ ] Event types correctly discriminated
- [ ] Timestamp ordering reasonable (within skew)
- [ ] Zone binding correct
- [ ] Event payload schema-validated

### AuditHead
- [ ] Quorum signatures required (n-f for CriticalWrite)
- [ ] Insufficient signatures rejected
- [ ] Signature from non-member rejected
- [ ] Signature set canonicalized (sorted by node_id)
- [ ] AuditHead references correct chain tip

### ZoneCheckpoint
- [ ] Binds audit_head correctly (id + seq)
- [ ] Binds revocation_head correctly (id + seq)
- [ ] Binds policy_head correctly
- [ ] Binds config_head correctly
- [ ] Quorum signatures valid
- [ ] seq is monotonic

### DecisionReceipt
- [ ] Content-addressed (stable ObjectId)
- [ ] reason_code is stable and enumerable
- [ ] evidence ObjectIds are present and resolvable
- [ ] Denied operations produce DecisionReceipt
- [ ] DecisionReceipt is in audit chain

### Fork Detection
- [ ] Same seq, different id → fork detected
- [ ] Fork detection halts checkpoint advancement
- [ ] Fork emits audit event
- [ ] Fork requires manual intervention
- [ ] No auto-resolution of forks

### TraceContext Propagation
- [ ] trace_id preserved through invoke → receipt → audit
- [ ] span_id correctly propagated
- [ ] Missing trace_id handled gracefully

### Explainability (fcp explain)
- [ ] DecisionReceipt can be fetched by operation_id
- [ ] Evidence chain can be traversed
- [ ] Reason code maps to human explanation
- [ ] AI recovery hints available where applicable

## Golden Vectors
- Valid AuditEvent chain segment
- AuditHead with quorum signatures
- ZoneCheckpoint binding all heads
- DecisionReceipt with evidence

## Property Tests
- Seq is always monotonic
- Hash linking is consistent
- No event can be removed without detection

## Fuzz Targets
- AuditEvent parsing
- AuditHead signature verification
- DecisionReceipt parsing

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "test_name": "...",
  "chain_seq": 123,
  "event_type": "...",
  "checkpoint_seq": 456,
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "reason_code": "..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Chain integrity verified
- Fork detection works
- DecisionReceipts are explainable

+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-upp69","title":"[LOW] fcp-graphql dedup keys on u64 DefaultHasher -- hypothetical collision serves wrong response","description":"crates/fcp-graphql/src/client.rs:656-660:

    fn hash_bytes(bytes: &[u8]) -> u64 {
        let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();
        bytes.hash(&mut hasher);
        hasher.finish()
    }

DefaultHasher is SipHash with a fixed seed. Two distinct request bodies that happen to produce the same u64 hash will be treated as the same in-flight dedup entry, so the second caller receives the response intended for the first.

For random inputs collisions require 2^32 entries (birthday bound), so in practice this is not a real risk. Adversarial collisions require knowing the hasher seed; DefaultHasher is not HashDoS-resistant.

Exploit surface requires: (1) attacker controls both request bodies going through the same GraphqlClient dedup map, (2) server returns different bodies for the two, (3) attacker can win the race to be first-in-map. Unusual but not impossible for a shared multi-tenant client.

Fix: key the dedup map on the actual Vec<u8> body or on a collision-resistant digest (blake3/sha256) instead of a 64-bit SipHash output.

Files: crates/fcp-graphql/src/client.rs:519 (key = hash_bytes(&body_bytes)), crates/fcp-graphql/src/client.rs:656-660 (hash_bytes).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T08:00:35.625725Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:00:35.625725Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ur6o","title":"SymbolEnvelope AEAD encrypt/decrypt + sender subkey derivation","description":"# Goal\nImplement FCP2 SymbolEnvelope AEAD encryption/decryption per spec (Section 4.1) and per-sender subkey derivation.\n\n# Scope\n- Implement per-sender subkey derivation (HKDF-SHA256 with zone_key_id salt, sender node id, sender_instance_id).\n- Implement SymbolEnvelope::encrypt/decrypt with deterministic nonces and fixed-size AAD using ZoneIdHash + zone_key_id + epoch.\n- Use ChaCha20-Poly1305 / XChaCha20-Poly1305 based on ZoneKeyAlgorithm.\n- Return ciphertext + auth_tag separately to match envelope layout.\n\n# Acceptance\n- Unit tests: AAD construction, nonce derivation, encrypt/decrypt roundtrip for both algorithms.\n- Reject mismatched zone_key_id on decrypt.\n- No unsafe; secrets not logged.\n","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add spectral routing score (effective resistance / Laplacian) as optional metric and log chosen path.
- Set gossip TTL from measured mixing-time; add convergence test that asserts bound.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use SmallVec for usually-small object/symbol lists to reduce allocations.
- Batch symbol requests and use Bytes for zero-copy in hot paths.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-uylsk","title":"[bug] fwc truth classification drift between catalog, readiness, and dispatch","description":"Fresh archaeology found that crates/fwc/src/catalog.rs and crates/fwc/src/readiness.rs disagree about command truth semantics, and crates/fwc/src/main.rs now disagrees with catalog.rs on real behavior. Examples: config, recipe, and pipeline have offline artifact subcommands in dispatch but are still classified as live-host-only in catalog.rs; do is emitted as offline-artifact in dispatch but still marked hybrid in catalog.rs; capabilities always emits offline-artifact envelopes even when augmenting with host metadata; preflight has live and offline branches in dispatch but is missing from catalog.rs. This can mislead intent and workflow availability inference plus agent-facing guidance built from the stale catalog table. Acceptance: align the canonical truth classification with actual dispatch behavior, add regression coverage so the tables cannot drift again, and keep host and offline guidance mechanically consistent.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:04:17.230588Z","created_by":"CreamReef","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:20:09.669734Z","closed_at":"2026-03-26T21:20:09.669479Z","close_reason":"Implemented truth-contract alignment; remote cargo verification blocked by RCH-E326","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","planner","readiness","truthfulness"],"comments":[{"id":2027,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-uylsk","author":"CreamReef","text":"Implemented `fwc` command truth-contract alignment across `catalog.rs` and `readiness.rs` for `context`, `mesh`, `agent`, `lifecycle`, `preflight`, `replay`, `compare`, `undo`, `approvals`, `bench`, `new`, `tail`, and `watch`, and reclassified `config`, `recipe`, and `pipeline` as hybrid to match actual dispatch behavior. Added regression coverage for the selected command set and readiness/catalog alignment. Local checks passed: `git diff --check -- crates/fwc/src/catalog.rs crates/fwc/src/readiness.rs` and `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/fwc/src/catalog.rs crates/fwc/src/readiness.rs`. Remote verification is currently blocked by `rch exec` dependency preflight `RCH-E326`, which attempted to fail open to a local build; that fallback was explicitly terminated to honor the repo instruction to offload heavy Cargo work through `rch`. Workspace `cargo fmt --check` also reports unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in `connectors/jira/src/client.rs` and `crates/fwc/src/main.rs`.","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:20:04Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-w82c","title":"[FCP2] Long-Running Operation Progress: Real-Time Feedback","description":"# [FCP2] Long-Running Operation Progress: Real-Time Feedback

## Goal
Enable agents to receive progress updates for long-running operations without blocking or polling.

## Problem
Some connector operations take significant time:
- Large file uploads/downloads
- Batch API calls
- Data migrations
- Complex queries

Agents currently have no visibility into progress - they either block waiting or timeout without knowing how close the operation was to completion.

## Solution: Progress Streaming

### Request Format
```jsonc
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": 1,
  "method": "tools/invoke",
  "params": {
    "tool": "fcp.storage.upload",
    "input": {
      "path": "/large-file.tar.gz",
      "destination": "s3://bucket/path"
    },
    "options": {
      "stream_progress": true,  // Request progress updates
      "progress_interval_ms": 500  // Min interval between updates
    }
  }
}
```

### Progress Notification Format
When `stream_progress: true`, the host sends JSON-RPC notifications:

```jsonc
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "method": "progress/update",
  "params": {
    "operation_id": "op_abc123",
    "request_id": 1,  // Correlates to original request
    "progress": {
      "phase": "uploading",  // Current phase name
      "current": 45000000,   // Current progress (bytes, items, etc.)
      "total": 100000000,    // Total expected (if known)
      "unit": "bytes",       // Unit of measurement
      "percentage": 45.0,    // Computed percentage
      "rate": 5000000,       // Rate per second (if applicable)
      "eta_ms": 11000,       // Estimated time remaining
      "message": "Uploading chunk 9 of 20"  // Human-readable status
    },
    "timestamp": "2026-01-22T18:00:00Z"
  }
}
```

### Phase Transitions
```jsonc
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "method": "progress/phase",
  "params": {
    "operation_id": "op_abc123",
    "request_id": 1,
    "from_phase": "preparing",
    "to_phase": "uploading",
    "phases_remaining": ["verifying", "completing"],
    "timestamp": "2026-01-22T18:00:00Z"
  }
}
```

### Implementation

```rust
pub struct ProgressTracker {
    operation_id: OperationId,
    request_id: RequestId,
    sender: mpsc::Sender<ProgressNotification>,
    interval: Duration,
    last_sent: Instant,
}

impl ProgressTracker {
    /// Report progress (throttled by interval)
    pub async fn report(&mut self, progress: Progress) -> Result<(), FcpError> {
        if self.last_sent.elapsed() >= self.interval {
            let notification = ProgressNotification {
                operation_id: self.operation_id.clone(),
                request_id: self.request_id,
                progress,
                timestamp: Utc::now(),
            };
            self.sender.send(notification).await?;
            self.last_sent = Instant::now();
        }
        Ok(())
    }

    /// Report phase transition (always sent)
    pub async fn transition(&mut self, from: &str, to: &str, remaining: &[&str]) -> Result<(), FcpError> {
        let notification = PhaseTransition {
            operation_id: self.operation_id.clone(),
            request_id: self.request_id,
            from_phase: from.to_string(),
            to_phase: to.to_string(),
            phases_remaining: remaining.iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect(),
            timestamp: Utc::now(),
        };
        self.sender.send(notification.into()).await?;
        Ok(())
    }
}

pub struct Progress {
    pub phase: String,
    pub current: u64,
    pub total: Option<u64>,
    pub unit: ProgressUnit,
    pub rate: Option<u64>,
    pub message: Option<String>,
}

pub enum ProgressUnit {
    Bytes,
    Items,
    Requests,
    Rows,
    Custom(String),
}
```

### Connector SDK Support

```rust
#[fcp_tool(streaming = true)]
async fn upload_file(
    ctx: &ToolContext,
    input: UploadInput,
    progress: ProgressTracker,  // Injected if stream_progress=true
) -> Result<UploadOutput, FcpError> {
    progress.transition("preparing", "uploading", &["verifying"]).await?;

    let mut uploaded = 0u64;
    for chunk in file.chunks(CHUNK_SIZE) {
        upload_chunk(chunk).await?;
        uploaded += chunk.len() as u64;
        progress.report(Progress {
            phase: "uploading".to_string(),
            current: uploaded,
            total: Some(file_size),
            unit: ProgressUnit::Bytes,
            rate: Some(calculate_rate()),
            message: Some(format!("Uploaded {} of {}", uploaded, file_size)),
        }).await?;
    }

    progress.transition("uploading", "verifying", &[]).await?;
    verify_upload().await?;

    Ok(UploadOutput { bytes_uploaded: uploaded })
}
```

### Tests

```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_progress_streaming_basic() {
    let harness = TestHarness::new().await;
    let (tx, mut rx) = mpsc::channel(100);

    harness.invoke_with_progress("test.slow_operation", json!({}), tx).await;

    let mut progress_updates = vec![];
    while let Some(update) = rx.recv().await {
        progress_updates.push(update);
    }

    assert!(progress_updates.len() > 0);
    // Progress should be monotonically increasing
    for window in progress_updates.windows(2) {
        assert!(window[1].progress.percentage >= window[0].progress.percentage);
    }
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_progress_interval_throttling() {
    // Progress updates respect interval setting
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_progress_phase_transitions() {
    // Phase transitions are always sent
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_progress_without_total() {
    // Works when total is unknown (indeterminate)
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_progress_cancellation() {
    // Progress stops when operation is cancelled
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tools can opt into progress reporting via SDK attribute
- [ ] Progress notifications are throttled by configurable interval
- [ ] Phase transitions are always sent (not throttled)
- [ ] Progress includes rate and ETA when calculable
- [ ] Works with both deterministic and indeterminate progress
- [ ] Progress stops immediately on cancellation
- [ ] Host aggregates progress for batch operations","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add metamorphic tests (encode->decode->encode) plus differential oracle for edge cases.
- Use sequential test budgets with e-value thresholds to stop early when safe.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Parallelize vector generation with deterministic seeds.
- Cache canonical forms to avoid repeated parse cost.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-w94","title":"fcp.airtable: View filtering and sorting support","description":"# fcp.airtable: View Filtering and Sorting Support

## Goal
Support Airtable views as a first-class query primitive (views encode filters/sorts that humans already curate).

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.views.list`
- `airtable.views.get`
- `airtable.views.records` (list records through a view)

## Key requirements
- Capability gated (`airtable.records.read` and/or `airtable.views.read`).
- Views must be treated as “query presets”:
  - do not silently broaden access beyond what the view returns
  - enforce field selection and page size bounds
- Sorting:
  - preserve deterministic ordering when paginating
- Filter formulas:
  - validate/escape inputs to prevent injection into filter expressions

## Tests
- Mock-only tests cover:
  - pagination stability under view sorting
  - malformed view configs
  - permission failures and rate limits

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agents can reliably paginate view results without duplicates.
- Filter and sort behavior matches Airtable semantics.
","notes":"Implemented first-class Airtable view primitives and tightened list query validation.

Delivered:
- added airtable.list_views, airtable.get_view, and airtable.list_view_records in connector introspection/invoke path
- list_records now resolves table/view/field selectors against schema and validates filter, bounds, and sort inputs before making Airtable requests
- manifest metadata and operation pools updated for the new view operations
- integration coverage added for view lookup, ambiguous view names, view-record pagination via offset, and filter/sort/view query encoding

Verification:
- rch exec -- cargo fmt --package fcp-airtable -- --check
- rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-airtable --all-targets --target-dir .target-airtable-w94

+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-wd6ei","title":"[HIGH] fcp-graphql dedup map leaks entries on caller cancel/panic","description":"crates/fcp-graphql/src/client.rs:519-538 (execute_bytes) implements in-flight request dedup as:

    let mut guard = state.inner.lock().await;
    if let Some(shared) = guard.get(&key).cloned() {
        drop(guard);
        return shared.await;
    }
    let future = async move { client.send_with_retry(payload, idempotent).await }
        .boxed()
        .shared();
    guard.insert(key, future.clone());
    drop(guard);
    let result = future.await;
    state.inner.lock().await.remove(&key); // <-- only on non-cancel non-panic completion
    return result;

Failure modes:

1. Caller cancel: if the outer task is cancelled or dropped at the `future.await` suspension point, the removal line is skipped. The Shared handle stays in `state.inner` indefinitely.

2. Panic: same -- the `remove` line is not unwind-safe.

3. Result caching beyond in-flight: once Shared has resolved, subsequent same-body callers get the cached result forever (not just while in-flight). A response cached for Query X at T is served to all future duplicates of X, potentially hours later. dedup-in-flight semantics are silently promoted to "dedup forever".

Compounding: memory usage grows monotonically with the number of unique request bodies that the owning caller cancelled on. No TTL, no eviction, no size cap.

Fix shape: RAII guard that removes the entry on drop, unconditionally:

    struct DedupGuard { state: Arc<DedupState>, key: u64 }
    impl Drop for DedupGuard { fn drop(&mut self) { /* blocking remove or spawn remove */ } }

Or use a DashMap<u64, Weak<Shared>> so stale entries self-clean when all Shared handles drop.

Files: crates/fcp-graphql/src/client.rs:519-538 (execute_bytes).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T08:00:24.687252Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:00:24.687252Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-weh1v","title":"GCP: implement service account JWT signing or gate with explicit error","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:47.545621Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T04:53:03.751662Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T04:53:03.751484Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","hardening","placeholders"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-weh1v","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ohw06","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:03.521810Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1943,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-weh1v","author":"jemanuel","text":"AFFECTED FILE: connectors/gcp/src/types.rs:53-54

CURRENT STATE:
The GcpAuth::ServiceAccount variant's token() method returns the private_key field directly as a bearer token placeholder. Comment says 'real JWT signing would be added later'. This means service_account auth mode silently sends a raw private key as a Bearer token to Google APIs — which is both a security risk (key leakage in transit) and functionally broken (Google rejects non-JWT bearer tokens).

The access_token and credential_id auth modes work correctly. Only service_account is affected.

FIX:
1. Implement proper JWT signing for service_account mode:
   - Build JWT header: {"alg":"RS256","typ":"JWT"}
   - Build JWT claims: {"iss":service_account_email, "scope":scopes, "aud":"https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token", "iat":now, "exp":now+3600}
   - Sign with RS256 using the private key
   - Exchange signed JWT for access token via https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token
2. Alternatively, gate service_account mode with an explicit error: 'GCP service_account JWT signing is not yet implemented. Use access_token or credential_id mode.'
3. The fcp-google-discovery crate already handles Google auth via GoogleAuthSelection/GoogleMaterializedAuth — consider whether GCP connector should use that substrate instead of its own auth.

RECOMMENDATION: Gate with explicit error (option 2) as an immediate fix, then evaluate whether the GCP connector should migrate to the shared Google auth substrate (option 3) as a follow-up.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- service_account mode either produces valid JWTs or returns a clear error (no raw private key in Bearer header)
- doctor() reports auth mode and warns if service_account is selected without JWT support
- Tests verify the error path for service_account mode","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-whkbp","title":"[FCP3/P2.1] Carve fcp-kernel out of broad execution buckets","description":"Background: execution lifecycle semantics currently bleed across fcp-core, fcp-host, and connector runtime code. V3 needs a dedicated kernel owner so invoke, drain, checkpoint, resume, budgeting, and execution form neutrality are not host inventions.

Scope: define and extract fcp-kernel as the owner of execution semantics. The contract should cover the lifecycle shared by host, SDK, and mesh placement decisions, including control points needed for supervision and failover without hard coding those semantics into the host. Make the owned execution vocabulary concrete by defining deterministic lifecycle fixtures, checkpoint state examples, structured runtime trace logs, and precise fields that later unit tests, integration tests, and end to end test scripts can assert directly.

Acceptance: fcp-kernel owns the execution model, downstream callers can target it directly, broad semantic buckets stop being the canonical source for execution meaning, and later verification work has precise fixtures and trace logs for regression diagnosis.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:04.207424Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-28T06:24:43.034954Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T06:24:43.034724Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["execution","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","kernel","mesh-first","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-whkbp","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6huk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:20.631596Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-whkbp","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-xn3h3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:20.864414Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2070,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-whkbp","author":"jemanuel","text":"SandyOak starting work on fcp-kernel extraction. Will define execution lifecycle types, checkpoint state, budget accounting, and extract them from fcp-core into a new fcp-kernel crate.","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:34:06Z"},{"id":2072,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-whkbp","author":"jemanuel","text":"SandyOak: Extended fcp-kernel with full execution domain re-exports (commit 6fd7dbae). Now covers: lease/execution authority, checkpoint/recovery, computation migration/state, budget/resource accounting, rate limiting, quorum/consensus, shutdown/drain. 13 tests verify all domains compile. Phase 1 (re-exports from fcp-core) is substantially complete. Phase 2 (move definitions into fcp-kernel and make fcp-core re-export from kernel) is the next step.","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:40:20Z"},{"id":2075,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-whkbp","author":"jemanuel","text":"WhiteCompass completed 2026-03-28: Created crates/fcp-kernel/ with 251-line facade re-exporting execution lifecycle types from fcp-core. Covers: InvokeRequest/Response/Status, Handshake, Simulate, FcpConnector trait + 5 archetype traits, LifecycleState machine (7 states), OperationInfo/Introspection, Health/SelfCheck, Lease/Checkpoint/Migration, Budget/RateLimit, Quorum/Consensus, Shutdown. 11 tests verify type accessibility. Build pending CI (workspace resolution exceeds 10min locally).","created_at":"2026-03-28T06:24:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-wke","title":"fcp.qdrant: Qdrant Vector Database Connector","description":"# fcp.qdrant - Qdrant Vector Database Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant connector for Qdrant (self-hosted or managed) supporting vector storage and semantic retrieval under strict zone isolation.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)
- `storage` (CRUD: collections/points)
- `knowledge` (semantic retrieval/search)

## Zone model
- Single-zone bound: one connector instance ↔ one `ZoneId`.
- Zone policies decide which datasets are accessible; the connector must never multiplex zones.

## External surface
### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only the configured Qdrant endpoint(s) and port(s) required by declared operations.

**Important:** host restrictions are expressed via NetworkConstraints, not capability IDs.

### Credential injection
- API key / bearer token (if used) via `CredentialId`.
- Optional TLS client auth via `CredentialId` (if required by deployment).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `qdrant.collections.manage` (create/delete/update collections)
- `qdrant.points.read`
- `qdrant.points.write`
- `qdrant.search.query`
- `qdrant.snapshots.manage` (Dangerous)

## Operations (initial)
- Collections: create/get/list/delete
- Points: upsert/get/delete/scroll
- Search: query / batch query

## Retry / idempotency
- Upsert by point id is naturally idempotent; safe retries are allowed.
- Delete operations must be receipt-bound and fail closed on uncertain outcomes.

## Tests
- Mock-only unit tests using wiremock.
- E2E compliance run validates:
  - default deny
  - NetworkConstraints enforcement
  - receipts/audit for writes

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic).
- No real Qdrant calls in unit tests.
